threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of the 'Underground'—the locals can spot the freshies a mile away.
Caught a girl arguing with a pigeon outside the Ritzy at 3am. She insisted it was her 'spirit animal.' Strange night in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I heard a street musician playing a remix of Berliner Luft on the U-Bahn at 3 AM while the city was half asleep.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Friedrichshain has its own 'Kiezkneipe' vibe, where you can find a pub hosting a 2 AM poetry slam, right next to a kebab stand playing 90s techno.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they know to pack an extra sweater for those freezing BART delays on a sunny day.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a saxophonist jamming at the Civic Center stop, Muni delayed yet again. Crowds just swayed and clapped, somehow made the wait feel shorter.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all tapas are created equal, but only locals know the best hidden tapas bars that serve dishes that aren't on any menu.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they stare at the metro map like it's a treasure map, lost and hopeful at the same time.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You know someone’s fresh when they’re still baffled by the lack of signal in the Underground. It’s like a rite of passage.
Last night on the 333, a guy was arguing with a bin about where to put his takeaway. Told the driver he'd be back for his 'real meal', and then just walked off.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Walking past the old Turkish market, I spotted a grandma haggling over tomatoes while the scent of fresh baklava filled the air. That's when it hit me.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a currywurst stand open at 3 AM, fiercely competing with a hipster café for the late-night crowd's attention.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they just shrug at a BART delay and say, 'At least it’s not raining today.' That’s real resilience.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tux on a Muni bus, clutching a bouquet—clearly late to a wedding. The bus hit a pothole, and he almost flew. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the 'locals' are in Plaça del Sol at all hours. Real Graciencs know the best chats happen in the corners of the quieter streets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they order a coffee—only tourists ask for it to go. Locals savor their café con leche, sitting at the counter.
